notes: SHIP v1.14.285 - ACTIVE SHIFT HERO 3b: hero verbs + BLOCKER wired to existing capture + LOG relocated (R2). Ship 3b per SHIP3B-SESSION-KICKOFF.md; R2 ratified. WHAT SHIPPED (redesign-only; ?legacy=1 byte-identical): (1) three hero verbs SCAN / LOG / BLOCKER inside .lens (the Active Shift hero), a .hverbs flex row of 60px gloved targets, uniform cyan. SCAN -> cmd_route('work','scan') (Work hosts; spine moves to WORK). LOG -> openLogSheet() (the SAME #log-sheet, same destination rule - no active deployment still blocks SEND; Ship 1 write path untouched). (2) BLOCKER -> blocker_quick(deploy_getActiveId()) - ONE DOOR per owner ruling: wired to the EXISTING blocker capture, NOT a new sheet. blocker_quick() gained an optional deployIdHint param (backward-compatible; existing no-arg callers unchanged) so the hero verb pre-scopes to the redesign ACTIVE deployment (deploy_getActiveId / ACTIVE_DEPLOYMENT_KEY - the same id LOG targets). KEY FINDING: blocker_quick previously derived scope only from activeContext_get() (last-touched deep context), which can DIVERGE from deploy_getActiveId(); with multiple deployments and no deep context it would show the deploy-picker instead of pre-scoping to the active one (R2 violation) - the hint fixes that. Write path (deploy_advancePhase -> phase.blockerNote, marks status='blocked', refreshes audit + rack rollup + TODAY blockers) UNTOUCHED. Capture -> deploy_countBlockers increments -> the BLOCKERS STAT (not a separate tile) reflects it on next cmd_render; the issues/blockers list door (issue_pageOpen) is unchanged. (3) LOG PILL RELOCATED: removed the temporary askrow .apill.ls-pill (Ship 1's trigger) - LOG now lives only in the hero. ONE LOG door. The askrow is left as ASK + RESERVED (the 2-tile rebalance is Ship 4a, explicitly out of 3b fence). REDESIGN-FITNESS (owner asked confirm-first): blocker_quick's modal (blocker_render @20374) is ALREADY a body-level bottom sheet (fixed inset:0, align-items:flex-end, scrim+backdrop-blur, tokenized) -> structurally redesign-fit, wired as-is per the one-door ruling (no restyle needed). OUT-OF-FENCE FINDINGS (Ship 4b, NOT touched here per kickoff 'no channel recolor'): the blocker modal is painted RED (#ff453a / rgba(255,69,58)) - off the R5 fault channel (should be MAG); AND the new BLOCKER verb is uniform cyan (its mag fault-channel accent + R2's 'hero grows the mag fault strip' are channel/emitter work = Ship 4b). GATES: node --check (only JS-structural edit = the blocker_quick hint param, so any check failure is unambiguous - law 3 honored), CSS brace balance, CRLF preserved, three-stamp .284->.285. DEVICE-VERIFY (John, iPhone): three verbs in the hero, one-handed gloved hits; SCAN enters scan flow (spine -> WORK); LOG opens the sheet, destination live, no-deployment still blocks SEND; askrow LOG pill GONE (exactly one LOG door); BLOCKER opens capture pre-scoped to the active deployment, send increments the BLOCKERS stat, dismiss-without-send changes nothing; no horizontal overflow; ?legacy=1 byte-identical. HARD STOP at device-verify. NEXT: Ship 4 (4a reserved-slot guard + askrow rebalance + 4b glow-normalize + NBA mag->vio + blocker modal red->mag + BLOCKER verb accent, bundled behind R5).
